name: Superman
resistance_profile: Bodyweight spinal extension from the floor; hardest at the top where the erectors are fully shortened under peak contraction.
cues: Reach long through the fingertips and toes
Lift with the low back and glutes, not by cranking the neck
Pause and squeeze at the top
execution_steps: Lie face down with the arms extended overhead and the legs straight.
Brace lightly and simultaneously lift the arms, chest, and legs off the floor.
Squeeze the low back and glutes at the top and hold briefly.
Lower everything back to the floor under control and repeat.
good_when: You want a no-equipment way to train the erectors directly
You need a gentle entry point for low-back strengthening
bad_when: You have pain with end-range lumbar extension
You need meaningful progressive overload — bodyweight resistance caps out quickly
common_errors: Bouncing off the floor with momentum
progressions: Add isometric holds at the top
Hold a light plate or wear ankle weights
regressions: Alternate lifting only the upper body or only the legs
